GitClaw recorded a channel-origin poll vote on the GitHub poll issue, then queued a provider-facing acknowledgement back to the mirrored channel thread. The poll issue contains the human-readable vote; this source receipt keeps poll IDs, vote IDs, choice text, participant text, notes, thread IDs, message IDs, and channel message bodies out of band.

### Follow-Up Delivery
- provider gateways read the poll vote acknowledgement with `gitclaw channel-outbox --channel <provider> --account-id <account> --issue-number <issue> --out <file>`
- provider gateways record sent acknowledgements with `gitclaw channel-delivery --channel <provider> --account-id <account> --issue-number <issue> --comment-id <comment> --external-message-id <message>`
- duplicate poll vote records are suppressed by `poll_id + vote_id`; duplicate acknowledgements are suppressed by `channel + notify_message_id`
- normal GitClaw conversation continues on the poll issue with the `gitclaw` label

@gitclaw /channels poll-vote --poll-id poll-vote-20260602T101858Z --message-id poll-vote-msg-20260602T101858Z --notify-message-id poll-vote-ack-20260602T101858Z --choice 1
Note: duplicate should not leak NOECHO_CHANNEL_POLL_VOTE_DUPLICATE_20260602T101858Z

GitClaw channel poll

Poll: #705
URL: https://github.com/AnandChowdhary/gitclaw/issues/705
Source: #704

Question:
Which channel poll vote path should ship for NOECHO_CHANNEL_POLL_VOTE_QUESTION_20260602T101858Z?

Options:
1. Ship the poll vote path
2. Keep the poll invite only

Vote in the linked GitHub poll or continue through the mirrored channel thread.
